STIPULATION TO JUDGMENT FILED BY PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA CURRY, LISSETTE

DISCOVERY 302, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FIRST SET OF DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS; PETITIONER'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ALTHOUGH THE MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION, AND THE DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION REPRESENT THAT THE DISCOVERY WAS SERVED ON THE CLAIMANT ON JANUARY 7, 2009, THOSE REPRESENTATIONS ARE INCONSISTENT WITH THE PROOF OF SERVICE EXECUTED BY CHLOE T. MOSQUEDA. MOREOVER, WHILE THE MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION STATES THAT "[T]O DATE, NO RESPONSES HAVE BEEN RECEIVED FROM CLAIMANT," THAT IS NOT WHAT THE SUPPORTING DECLARATION SAYS. IN THE DECLARATION, COUNSEL REPRESENTS ONLY THAT "[T]O DATE, PETITIONER HAS NOT RECEIVED COMPLETE ANSWERS TO THESE DISCOVERY REQUESTS." DECLARATION OF PAMELA A. UNDERWOOD AT PARA. 3, EMPHASIS ADDED. BECAUSE THE SUBSTANTIVE AND PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS FOR MOTIONS COMPELLING RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY DIFFER, DEPENDING UPON WHETHER INADEQUATE ANSWERS WERE SERVED, OR NO ANSWERS AT ALL WERE PROVIDED, AND BECAUSE THE PAPERS SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION ARE IN CONFLICT ON THIS ISSUE, THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. COURT TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: RICHARD GROTCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
Mtn To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Docs
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The Motion by Petitioner, People of the State of California to Compel Responses to the First Set of Demand for Production and Inspection of Documents; First Set of Judicial Council Form Interrogatories and First Set of Special Interrogatories is GRANTED. Claimant Lissette Curry is ORDERED to serve complete, verified responses, without objections within 20 days of notice of this order. Failure to serve said responses will subject Claimant Lissette Curry to terminating sanctions and forfeiture of his claim to the subject $3,500.00 upon further Motion by Petitioner. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Peter J. Van Zandt; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner's First Set Of Special Interrogatories is DENIED without prejudice. Although the memorandum in support of the motion, and the declaration in support of the motion represent that the discovery was served on the claimant on January 7, 2009, those representations are inconsistent with the proof of service executed by Chloe T. Mosqueda. Moreover, while the memorandum in support of the motion states that "[t]o date, no responses have been received from Claimant," that is not what the supporting declaration says. In the declaration, counsel represents only that "[t]o date, Petitioner has not received complete answers to these discovery requests." Declaration of Pamela A. Underwood at para. 3, emphasis added. Because the substantive and procedural requirements for motions compelling responses to discovery differ, depending upon whether inadequate answers were served, or no answers at all were provided, and because the papers submitted in support of this motion are in conflict on this issue, the motion is DENIED without prejudice. Court to prepare order. Judge Pro Tem: Richard Grotch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported
